page: Vultr vbm-72c-480gb-gh200-gpu is a bare metal server featuring the NVIDIA Grace CPU architecture. It provides 72 dedicated physical cores based on the Arm64 Neoverse V2 design, operating at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz with one thread per core. The system is equipped with 480.0 GB of system memory, yielding 6.67 GB of RAM per core.

This configuration integrates an NVIDIA Hopper GH200 GPU with 96 GB of unified VRAM. Local storage consists of a 960 GB NVMe SSD, and the server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. The tight integration of the Grace CPU and Hopper GPU via high-bandwidth interconnects defines the hardware profile of this bare metal instance.

From a resource density perspective, the server offers high compute and memory capacity tailored for specialized workloads. The bare metal deployment eliminates virtualization overhead, though the lack of hyperthreading means execution is strictly limited to one thread per physical core. This hardware profile is designed for large-scale artificial intelligence workloads, deep learning training, LLM inference, and high-performance computing tasks requiring massive GPU memory and dedicated Arm64 CPU resources.
description: Vultr vbm-72c-480gb-gh200-gpu is a bare metal server utilizing the NVIDIA Grace CPU and Hopper GPU architectures. It features 72 dedicated physical Arm64 Neoverse V2 cores running at 3.1 GHz, paired with 480.0 GB of system memory. The server integrates a single NVIDIA GH200 GPU with 96 GB of VRAM, alongside 960 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating without virtualization overhead, this bare metal instance provides dedicated hardware resources for demanding computational tasks. The architectural synergy between the 72-core Grace CPU and the GH200 GPU makes this configuration suitable for large-scale artificial intelligence, machine learning model training, LLM inference, and complex scientific simulations.
